title: onobrychis alba subsp. laconica
intro: Onobrychis alba subsp. laconica, also known as Laconian white clover or Laconian sainfoin, is a subspecies of the white clover plant native to Greece. This plant is a legume with medicinal properties, primarily containing isoflavones and coumarins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis alba subsp. laconica include its potential to reduce cardiovascular disease risk, alleviate menopausal symptoms, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Onobrychis alba subsp. laconica are typically made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
